mnemonics.title: FILE ATTRIBUTES
mnemonics.content: NAME-IDENTIFIER-TYPE-LOCATION-EXTRAS (NITLE): Name, Identifier, Type, Location, Time/Date, Last modified, Encryption, Size, Protection.

mnemonics.title: FILE OPERATIONS
mnemonics.content: COOKS RDN (Create, Open, Close, Kill/Close, Seek, Read, Delete/Write, eXecute). Some use: Create, Delete, Open, Close, Read, Write, Seek.

mnemonics.title: FILE ACCESS METHODS
mnemonics.content: SAS (Sequential, Indexed, Direct/Hashed). Remember: S-I-D - Simple, Indexed, Direct.

acronyms.usage: A file system that uses a table to track which clusters are allocated to each file.
Modern file system in Windows with advanced features like encryption and compression.
A marker indicating that no more data can be read from a file.
A data structure in memory that stores information about an open file.
System software that manages hardware and provides file system services.

visualTips.visualization: Think of a cassette tape where you must fast-forward or rewind to reach a specific song.
Think of a DVD or CD where you can jump directly to any chapter without playing previous ones.
Think of a book index at the back - you look up a topic and it points you to the exact page number.
Think of a library card catalog system - the card number (descriptor) lets you find the actual book.
Think of a water bucket - you fill it from the tap (memory) and pour into the pool (disk) less frequently.

analogies.analogy: Like a physical file folder in an office cabinet - it holds related documents together and has a label (name) on it.
Like a drawer in a filing cabinet - it organizes multiple files together.
Like a mailing address - tells the OS exactly where to find the file in the storage hierarchy.
Like the label on a jar - tells you what's inside (e.g., .exe = executable program, .txt = text document).
Like a tray near a printer - collects multiple pages before printing, reducing printer trips.
